ai_agent.role: Creates and manages construction budgets for 27+ home models (spec and custom), handles vendor quotation process, maintains cost matrices (materials + labor + overhead), produces R3 pre-contract estimates, manages MEP payment splits (FOCO 40-40-20, ECOWAN 40-30-30), and tracks budget-to-actual variance.
workflow_chains: takeoff_received -> apply_unit_prices -> county_adjustments -> samuel_review -> budget_finalized -> notify_scheduling_financeiro
custom_design_received -> request_quotes_3_vendors -> collect_quotes -> compare_prices -> samuel_approval -> budget_creation -> client_presentation
r3_estimate_request -> cost_per_sqft_lookup -> category_coefficient -> present_to_client -> if_contract_signed -> convert_to_full_budget

staff.name: Felipe Nery
staff.role: Budget Lead / Estimating Engineer - overall budget creation and management for spec and custom homes, MEP payment split management, vendor negotiation, final budget review coordination

staff.name: Ana Helena Torres
staff.role: Budget/Quotation team - estimate-to-PO integration in BuilderTrend (manual process), quotation collection, Financeiro coordination for payment verification

staff.name: Pedro Henrique Cordeiro
staff.role: Quotation Specialist - vendor contact for quotes (2-3 quotes per service), price table management, quote status tracking with color-coded spreadsheets, CAS Creed vs CMEX nomenclature mapping

staff.name: Rodrigo Cordeiro
staff.role: Concrete Budget Specialist - concrete-specific budget calculations, slab and lintel concrete volumes, concrete vendor relationships

core_knowledge.description: Lakeshore maintains budget templates for 27+ standardized home models. Each template is replicable across houses of the same model. Templates use POs in cost code order. Budget varies by county (Marion, Citrus, Charlotte) due to different permit fees, impact fees, wind speed requirements, utility costs, and labor rates. Budget is finalized by Samuel Torres and altered only when material/labor prices change.

The budget is built on a cost matrix that combines material quantities (from takeoff), labor costs (from sub quotes), and overhead (insurance, permits, office costs). Each cost code has specific calculation methodology. The matrix is the core tool for both spec and custom home budgets.

R3 (Revision 3 of cost model) is an initial budget estimate created before contract signing, based on cost per square foot of recent similar projects (~$250/sqft). Used for client negotiation and contract pricing. Variables like terrain (final grading, septic connection) affect precision. R3 must convert to full budget when contract is signed. Samuel Torres manages R3 creation.

Two major MEP subcontractors have different payment split structures: FOCO pays 40% at rough, 40% at trim, 20% at final. ECOWAN pays 40% at rough, 30% at trim, 30% at final. Direct unit price comparison between vendors is misleading without considering these payment terms. Both splits must be correctly registered in POs for Financeiro processing.

Quotation tracking uses color codes: YELLOW = pending/needs review (requested not received), WHITE = standard/received (awaiting comparison), ORANGE = all received/needs attention (ready for Sam's review), GREEN = approved/within budget (Sam approved), RED = over budget/rejected/incoherent (Sam flagged). Sam has final approval on all quotes.

Budget estimates are expected to be within 10% of actual costs. Variance greater than 10% triggers review by Samuel Torres. The ~10% natural variation in takeoff quantities across different subs is considered acceptable. For custom homes, variance is typically higher due to client specification changes.

Budget is organized into 19 cost categories: 1-Contract, 2-Permit, 3-Utilities, 4-Land Preparation, 5-Foundation, 6-Plumbing, 7-Electric, 8-Masonry, 9-Roofing/Framing, 10-Windows/Ext Doors, 11-Doors, 12-Mechanical, 13-Finishing, 14-Exterior Finishing, 15-Specialties, 16-Final Clean, 17-Upgrades, 18-Variances, 19-Lot Variances. Each code has specific sub-items and calculation methods.

Samuel Torres (Sam) has final approval authority on all budgets and quotes. He reviews values based on experience and market knowledge. Red-flagged items are values he considers incoherent or over budget. His approval (green status) is required before any budget is finalized or any quote is accepted.

daily_workflows.name: Spec Home Budget Creation
steps: Receive plans and specifications from Design Office (Camila)
Use replicable templates with POs in cost code order
Apply existing quotations (same materials and prices per model)
Adjust county-specific fees (Marion, Citrus, Charlotte impact fees, permit fees, utility costs)
Samuel Torres reviews final values based on experience
Budget finalized; locked unless material/labor price changes
daily_workflows.frequency: Per new model; updated when prices change
critical_decisions: Whether price changes warrant template update across all models

daily_workflows.name: Custom Home Budget Creation
steps: Receive Design Book and Takeoff from Camila/Projetos
Request individual quotations from at least 3 vendors per service
Detail all items and costs including special features (pool, premium finishes)
Define allowances and 'by owner' items with Camila before contract
Samuel and Camila approve initial budget (R3 estimate)
After contract signing, PMs (Daniel, Cris, Edson) approve real budget with sub quotes
critical_decisions: Allowance vs fixed value for finishing items
Which vendors to request quotes from
R3 estimate precision (cost per sqft coefficient selection)

daily_workflows.name: Vendor Quotation Process
steps: Contact vendors for material and service prices via email and WhatsApp
Collect 2-3 quotations per service for competitive pricing
Record quotes in color-coded spreadsheet (Yellow=pending, White=received, Orange=all received)
Present compiled quotes to Samuel Torres for negotiation leverage and review
Samuel reviews: marks green (approved) or red (rejected/incoherent)
Approved quotes finalized (green status)
daily_workflows.frequency: Per project (custom) or per price update cycle (spec)
critical_decisions: Vendor selection based on price, reliability, and payment terms
Volume negotiation strategy for repeated orders

daily_workflows.name: Unit Price Material Calculation
steps: Receive vendor price table with unit prices (e.g., DZ Blocks - regular block $1.89)
Receive quantity from Takeoff team (Gabriela's Globin + ChatGPT estimation)
Multiply: quantity from takeoff x unit price from vendor table
Record final calculated value in project spreadsheet
Route to budget for inclusion
critical_decisions: Which vendor price table to use when multiple exist
Whether quantity variance from takeoff is acceptable

daily_workflows.name: MEP Payment Split Registration
steps: Determine if project is Full MAP based on Permits definition
Identify MEP contractor (FOCO or ECOWAN) for the project
Apply correct payment split: FOCO 40-40-20 or ECOWAN 40-30-30
Register specific deductions when applicable (e.g., $679.01 for non-provided bathtub)
Create POs with correct split values
Monitor that invoices do not exceed PO values
critical_decisions: Correct payment split application per contractor

features_used: Budget creation per model with 19 cost categories and 206 line items
Estimate templates linked to models (values frozen on project creation)
Budget vs projected vs actual cost comparison
Cost code-based organization matching BuilderTrend structure
Automatic approval when order value <= estimate; manual approval when exceeded
api_endpoints: /budget/{project_id} - GET project budget with cost code breakdown

features_used: Take-off page for quantity definition per model
Supply type classification: Stocked, Quoted, By Sub, Non-stock
Vendor Takeoff feature (Enable Vendor Takeoff flag)
Service catalog with cost codes mapped to trades
Price table registration for vendor catalogs

handoffs.trigger: Budget approved by Samuel Torres and ready for client presentation
handoffs.data_exchanged: Approved budget (condensed format for custom homes showing categories not line items), contract pricing, allowance definitions
handoffs.impact: Client Relations uses approved budget to communicate pricing to client. Inaccurate or delayed budget blocks contract signing.

handoffs.data_exchanged: PO values as payment reference, budget totals by cost code, MEP payment split schedules (FOCO/ECOWAN)
handoffs.impact: Financeiro uses budget values to verify invoices against POs. Incorrect budget = incorrect payment verification.

handoffs.data_exchanged: Budget as reference for material ordering, vendor selections, quantity approvals
handoffs.impact: Scheduling uses budget to plan material procurement timing and verify order amounts

handoffs.data_exchanged: Approved vendor list, material specifications, quantities, and approved prices
handoffs.impact: Purchasing uses budget as basis for all material purchases

handoffs.data_exchanged: Selected vendor quotations as basis for formal contracts
handoffs.impact: Contracts formalize the pricing negotiated by Budget team

handoffs.trigger: Takeoff quantities completed for model or project
handoffs.data_exchanged: Takeoff PDF with material quantities by category, design book, spec sheet, floor plans
handoffs.impact: Budget cannot be created without takeoff quantities. Currently no automatic notification when takeoff changes.

handoffs.trigger: Permit application stage - Full MAP determination
handoffs.data_exchanged: County assignment, Full MAP determination, permit fee schedule
handoffs.impact: County assignment affects budget template selection and fee calculations

pain_points.description: Rapid material price changes in the US market. Material costs and tariffs change quickly, causing invoices to have different values than POs. This creates cascading problems in Financeiro and requires constant budget revision.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual monitoring of price changes; reactive budget updates when invoices arrive with different amounts
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated price monitoring with threshold alerts (e.g., >5% change triggers budget review notification); vendor price table auto-update from periodic quotes

pain_points.description: Manual estimate-to-PO integration in BuilderTrend. Ana Helena spends hours daily manually transferring approved budget estimates into PO entries in BuilderTrend. This is the critical bottleneck in the budget team.
pain_points.current_workaround: Ana Helena performs manual entry for each PO; cross-references between spreadsheet and BuilderTrend
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated propagation from approved budget to PO creation in the construction management system

pain_points.description: Budget not ready before permit is issued. Budget should ideally be completed before permit issuance to ensure financial viability, but frequently lags behind, allowing construction to start without confirmed budget.
pain_points.current_workaround: Construction proceeds with preliminary estimates; budget finalized during early construction phases
pain_points.proposed_solution: Budget completion as a gating requirement for permit payment release; dashboard showing budget vs permit status alignment

pain_points.description: Knowledge concentration in Samuel Torres. Final approval authority, R3 estimation methodology, vendor negotiation strategy, and cost-per-sqft benchmarks are concentrated in Samuel's experience with no formal documentation.
pain_points.current_workaround: All critical decisions routed through Samuel; team cannot proceed without his availability
pain_points.proposed_solution: Document Samuel's decision frameworks, approval criteria, and benchmark data. Create decision support system with historical cost data and approval rules.

pain_points.description: POs registered out of order in old BuilderTrend system. POs were cadastered randomly without following cost code sequence, making it difficult to compare against the budget spreadsheet.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual sorting and cross-referencing between systems
pain_points.proposed_solution: Enforce cost code ordering in PO creation; automatic sequencing by cost code

pain_points.description: Duplicated functions: Energy Calculation requested by both Budget and Permits departments, sometimes resulting in dual vendor charges.
pain_points.current_workaround: Verbal coordination to avoid duplicate requests
pain_points.proposed_solution: Centralized document request system where one department requests and all consuming departments receive

pain_points.description: Communication with subs is challenging. Many subcontractors have limited technology skills and rely on WhatsApp/phone. Responses come through untrackable channels.
pain_points.current_workaround: Email and WhatsApp used interchangeably; Pedro and Ana Helena follow up manually
pain_points.proposed_solution: Vendor portal with simplified interface for quote submission; email fallback with automatic import

pain_points.description: Takeoff changes do not automatically notify budget team. When Gabriela updates a takeoff, the budget team continues working with outdated quantities until manually informed.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual notification via email or verbal communication (often delayed)
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated notification when takeoff document is updated; change summary highlighting affected budget categories

tribal_knowledge.description: Samuel Torres holds the institutional knowledge for budget approval decisions. He knows acceptable price ranges for every trade, when vendor quotes are 'incoherent' (red flag), volume discount thresholds for major vendors, and cost-per-sqft benchmarks by project type and county. His approval framework is entirely experience-based with no documented decision criteria.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Critical - no one else in the company can validate budget reasonableness or negotiate with the same effectiveness

tribal_knowledge.description: Historical cost data showing average construction cost per square foot by model type, county, and finishing level. Used for R3 estimates (~$250/sqft baseline). Includes knowledge of which project categories consistently run over or under budget and why.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: High - R3 estimates would lose accuracy; custom home pricing would be unreliable

tribal_knowledge.description: Knowledge of which vendors offer volume discounts, at what thresholds they trigger, and how to structure orders to maximize savings. Includes understanding of FOCO vs ECOWAN payment terms and their impact on total cost comparison.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: High - company would lose negotiating leverage and potentially pay higher prices

tribal_knowledge.description: Detailed knowledge of FOCO and ECOWAN contract structures, including which deductions are acceptable (e.g., $679.01 for non-provided bathtub), how to verify invoice amounts against PO splits, and historical patterns of change orders from MEP contractors.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: High - incorrect payment splits could lead to overpayment or contractor disputes

Volume pricing negotiation basics
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Color code meanings, quote comparison process, vendor nomenclature, approval workflow

R3 estimate creation using cost-per-sqft benchmarks
Allowance vs fixed value decision framework
Client-facing condensed budget vs internal detailed budget
Managing budget revisions during construction
Change order impact assessment
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: R3 creation, allowance logic, revision workflow, change order process

topics: FOCO payment structure: 40-40-20 across three milestones
ECOWAN payment structure: 40-30-30 across three milestones
Full MAP determination from Permits
Invoice verification against PO splits
Deduction documentation and application
MEP control spreadsheet usage
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Payment split structures, deduction rules, PO verification

compliance_requirements.requirement: Budget must be approved by Samuel Torres before contract pricing is presented to client
compliance_requirements.verification: Samuel's green approval status documented in quotation spreadsheet before budget release

compliance_requirements.requirement: At least 2-3 vendor quotations must be obtained per service for competitive pricing
compliance_requirements.regulation: Internal procurement policy - competitive bidding requirement
compliance_requirements.verification: Quotation spreadsheet shows multiple vendor quotes per service with comparison

compliance_requirements.requirement: Budget estimate values frozen when linked to project - price changes only affect future projects
compliance_requirements.verification: Const App enforces value freeze on project creation; changes to base data do not retroactively update

compliance_requirements.requirement: MEP payment splits must match contracted terms exactly (FOCO 40-40-20, ECOWAN 40-30-30)
compliance_requirements.verification: PO values match contracted split percentages; invoices verified against PO before payment

compliance_requirements.requirement: Orders exceeding estimated budget require project manager approval
compliance_requirements.regulation: Internal financial control - exceeded cost approval
compliance_requirements.verification: System flags orders above estimate; PM approval documented before procurement

key_metrics.target: < 10% variance for spec homes; < 15% for custom
key_metrics.measurement: Final actual cost / initial budget estimate ratio per completed project

key_metrics.target: 100% of budgets finalized before permit payment release
key_metrics.measurement: Percentage of projects with finalized budget at permit ready-to-issue stage

key_metrics.target: < 5 business days from quote request to all quotes received (orange status)
key_metrics.measurement: Days from first vendor contact to all quotes received per service

key_metrics.metric: Cost approval rate for exceeding costs
key_metrics.measurement: Approved exceeding cost requests / total exceeding cost requests

key_metrics.target: < 5% average variance between PO value and final invoice
key_metrics.measurement: Average absolute difference between PO amount and paid invoice amount

key_metrics.target: R3 within 15% of final actual cost for custom homes
key_metrics.measurement: Final actual cost / R3 estimate ratio for custom projects
